title: dichanthelium sabulorum
intro: Dichanthelium sabulorum, also known as Sabine's rosette grass or Sand Dropseed, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dichanthelium sabulorum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate stress. The plant is typically prepared as a tea by infusing the dried leaves and roots in hot water, and can also be used in tinctures and salves for topical application.
